meta.title: Validation Rules Engine — Design Compliance Checks
meta.description: Every validation rule the software must check. Each rule has: condition, check logic, error message, code reference, severity. The backend reads this JSON and executes checks programmatically.

validation_rules.name: Smoke Detector — Smooth Ceiling Spacing (NFPA)

rule.description: Distance between any two adjacent spot-type smoke detectors must not exceed 30 ft (9.1m) on smooth ceilings
rule.note: The 30 ft spacing is measured along the diagonal of the square grid. Side-by-side spacing = 21 ft (6.4m). This is a common mistake.

calculation.method: For each pair of adjacent detectors on the same floor/zone, calculate Euclidean distance between their (x,y) positions. If distance > max_spacing → FAIL.
calculation.apply_correction: Multiply max_spacing by ceiling_height_correction_factor if ceiling > 10 ft

validation_rules.error_message: Smoke detector spacing violation: {actual_distance}m exceeds maximum {max_spacing}m (NFPA 72 Section 17.6.3.1)
validation_rules.resolution: Reduce spacing between detectors or add additional detector(s)

validation_rules.name: Smoke Detector — Wall Distance (NFPA)

rule.description: Distance from any smoke detector to the nearest wall must not exceed half the nominal spacing = 15 ft (4.6m)

calculation.method: For each detector, find minimum distance to any wall boundary of the room/floor. If min_wall_dist > max_wall_distance → FAIL.
calculation.requires: Room boundary polygon or floor boundary polygon

validation_rules.error_message: Detector {device_tag} is {actual}m from wall — exceeds maximum {max}m (NFPA 72 Section 17.6.3.1)
validation_rules.resolution: Move detector closer to wall or add additional detector

validation_rules.name: Smoke Detector — Corridor Spacing (NFPA)

rule.description: In corridors, detectors spaced max 30 ft (9.1m) on center, within 5 ft (1.5m) of end walls

calculation.method: Check corridor width ≤ 20 ft. Check center-to-center distance ≤ 30 ft. Check distance from first/last detector to end walls ≤ 5 ft.

validation_rules.error_message: Corridor detector spacing: {issue_type} — detector {device_tag} violates NFPA 72 Section 17.6.3.1.1
validation_rules.resolution: Adjust detector positions or add detector(s)

validation_rules.name: Smoke Detector — BS 5839 Spacing

rule.description: Nominal spacing between smoke detectors = 7.5m (BS 5839-1). Coverage radius = 5.3m. Max wall distance = 3.75m.

calculation.method: Check distance between adjacent detectors ≤ 7.5m. Check distance from detector to nearest wall ≤ 3.75m.

validation_rules.error_message: Detector spacing: {actual}m exceeds BS 5839 maximum {max}m (BS 5839-1 Section 22.3)
validation_rules.resolution: Reduce spacing or add detector(s)

validation_rules.name: Smoke Detector — BS 5839 Corridor Bonus

rule.description: In corridors less than 2m wide, spacing can be increased by 50% to 11.25m (BS 5839-1 Clause 22.3)

calculation.method: If corridor width ≤ 2.0m, apply bonus spacing of 11.25m. If > 2.0m, use normal 7.5m spacing.

validation_rules.message: INFO: Corridor {corridor_name} is {width}m wide — bonus spacing (11.25m) applies per BS 5839-1 Clause 22.3

validation_rules.name: Heat Detector — Spacing (NFPA)

rule.description: Heat detector listed spacing = 50 ft (15.2m) on smooth ceilings. Max wall distance = 25 ft (7.6m).
rule.note: Some heat detectors have different listed spacing — use manufacturer data when available.

calculation.method: Same as smoke detector spacing check, but using heat detector values. Check for ceiling height corrections (heat detectors have stricter height limits).

validation_rules.error_message: Heat detector spacing: {actual}m exceeds maximum {max}m (NFPA 72 Section 17.6.4)
validation_rules.resolution: Reduce spacing or add heat detector(s)

validation_rules.name: Heat Detector — Spacing (BS 5839)

rule.description: BS 5839 heat detector nominal spacing = 5.3m. Coverage radius = 3.75m. Max wall distance = 2.65m.
rule.note: BS 5839 heat spacing is MUCH tighter than NFPA — this is a significant difference designers often miss.

calculation.method: Check distance between adjacent heat detectors ≤ 5.3m. Check wall distance ≤ 2.65m.

validation_rules.error_message: Heat detector spacing: {actual}m exceeds BS 5839 maximum {max}m (BS 5839-1 Section 22.4)
validation_rules.resolution: Reduce spacing or add heat detector(s)

validation_rules.name: Ceiling Height Exceeds Detector Maximum

rule.description: Point-type smoke detectors should not be installed on ceilings higher than 30 ft (9.1m). Above this, use beam detectors or aspirating systems.
rule.recommendation: For ceilings > 10.5m: consider beam or ASD (BS 5839). For > 30ft: use beam or ASD (NFPA).

calculation.method: Compare floor ceiling_height_m against device max_ceiling_height_m. If ceiling > max → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Ceiling height {height}m exceeds maximum {max}m for {device_type} — use beam detector or aspirating system (NFPA 72 Section 17.6.3.3.1)

validation_rules.name: Ceiling Height Correction Not Applied

rule.description: When ceiling height exceeds 10 ft (3m), detector spacing must be reduced according to height correction table.
rule.correction_table_source: NFPA 72 / BS 5839 ceiling height correction factors

calculation.method: If ceiling_height > 10ft, check that spacing between detectors has been multiplied by the appropriate correction factor. If spacing was NOT corrected → WARNING.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Ceiling height is {height}m but spacing does not appear to be corrected for height. Verify that height correction factor has been applied (NFPA 72 Section 17.6.3.3.1)

validation_rules.name: Detector Too Close to HVAC Diffuser

rule.description: Detectors should not be placed within 500mm of HVAC supply air diffusers to prevent false alarms and delayed detection.

calculation.method: If HVAC diffuser positions are known on the floor plan, check distance from each detector to nearest diffuser. If < 0.5m → WARNING.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Detector {device_tag} is within 0.5m of HVAC diffuser — risk of false alarm (BS 5839-1 Clause 22.2.2)

validation_rules.name: Smoke Detector in Kitchen/Bathroom

rule.description: Smoke detectors should NOT be installed in kitchens or bathrooms due to nuisance alarms from cooking steam and moisture.
rule.recommendation: Use heat detector (135°F/57°C or 190°F/88°C) instead of smoke detector

calculation.method: If room_type is kitchen/bathroom/shower AND device is smoke_detector → WARNING.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Smoke detector in {room_type} ({room_name}) — use heat detector instead to avoid false alarms

validation_rules.name: Detector in Dead Air Space

rule.description: Avoid placing detectors in dead air spaces: ceiling corners, peaks of sloped ceilings, behind deep beams, above obstructions.
dead_air_spaces: corner where two walls meet ceiling
peak of sloped ceiling > 3ft from detector
behind beam deeper than 10% of ceiling height

calculation.method: Check if detector is within 600mm of ceiling peak (sloped) or in beam pocket (beamed). Visual check — flag for engineer review.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Detector {device_tag} may be in a dead air space — verify placement (best practice)

validation_rules.name: Pull Station Near Exit (NFPA)

rule.description: Manual fire alarm boxes must be located within 5 ft (1.5m) of every exit doorway on every floor.

calculation.method: For each exit door on the floor plan, check that there is a pull station within 1.5m (5ft). If any exit has no pull station within range → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Exit door on {floor_name} has no pull station within {max}m — required per NFPA 72 Section 17.14.1
validation_rules.resolution: Add pull station within 1.5m of the exit door

validation_rules.name: Pull Station Travel Distance (NFPA)

rule.description: Maximum travel distance to nearest manual station = 200 ft (61m) measured along normal path of travel.

calculation.method: Calculate shortest path from any point on floor to nearest pull station along corridors/walkways. If any point > 61m → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Maximum travel distance {actual}m to nearest pull station exceeds {max}m (NFPA 72 Section 17.14.1.3)
validation_rules.resolution: Add pull station(s) to reduce travel distance

validation_rules.name: Pull Station Mounting Height (NFPA)

rule.description: Pull station operable part must be mounted at 3.5 ft to 4.5 ft (1.07m to 1.37m) above floor.

calculation.method: Check mounting_height_m of each pull station. If outside range → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Pull station {device_tag} mounting height {actual}m is outside required range {min}-{max}m (NFPA 72 Section 17.14.1.2)

validation_rules.name: Pull Station Travel Distance (BS 5839)

rule.description: Maximum travel distance to nearest manual call point = 30m (45m in exceptional circumstances). BS 5839 is much stricter than NFPA.

calculation.method: Calculate shortest path from any point to nearest call point. If > 30m → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Travel distance {actual}m exceeds BS 5839 maximum {max}m (BS 5839-1 Clause 19.1.2)
validation_rules.resolution: Add call point(s)

validation_rules.name: Audible Level Below Minimum

rule.description: Audible notification must produce minimum 75 dBA OR 15 dBA above ambient, whichever is greater, at ear level throughout occupiable spaces.

calculation.method: For each point in the floor plan, check that combined sound level from all audible devices ≥ required minimum. Uses inverse-square law: dBA_at_point = dBA_source - 20×log10(distance/1m).
calculation.note: This is an approximate calculation — field measurement required for final verification.

validation_rules.error_message: Audible level {actual} dBA at {location} is below minimum {min} dBA (NFPA 72 Section 18.4.3)
validation_rules.resolution: Add or relocate notification appliance(s), or increase output setting

validation_rules.name: Strobe Candela Below Room Requirement

rule.description: Minimum strobe candela = max(0.0375 × room_area_sqft, room_area_sqft / 20, 15 cd). Maximum = 110 cd (wall-mount) or 177 cd (ceiling-mount).

calculation.method: Calculate room area in sqft. Compute required_cd. Check that installed strobe(s) total candela ≥ required_cd. If required > max for single device → need multiple devices.

validation_rules.error_message: Strobe candela {actual} cd in room {room_name} ({area} sqft) is below required {required} cd (NFPA 72 Section 18.5.5.4)
validation_rules.resolution: Increase strobe candela setting or add additional strobe(s)

validation_rules.name: Notification Missing in Occupiable Space

rule.description: Every occupiable space must have both audible and visible notification (unless code exception applies).
rule.requirement: Check every room flagged as requiring notification has at least one audible device AND one visual device.

calculation.method: For each room with requires_notification=true, check that devices array includes at least one device from notification category. If no notification device → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Room {room_name} requires notification but has no notification devices installed (NFPA 72 Section 18.4)
validation_rules.resolution: Add horn-strobe or speaker-strobe in the room

validation_rules.name: NAC Current Exceeds Panel Limit

rule.description: Total current draw on a NAC must not exceed the panel's maximum NAC current rating.

calculation.method: Sum alarm_current_mA of all devices on the NAC. Compare against panel max_nac_current_per_circuit. If sum > max → FAIL.

validation_rules.error_message: NAC {circuit_label}: total load {total} mA exceeds panel limit {max} mA — circuit overloaded
validation_rules.resolution: Add another NAC circuit or reduce device count on this circuit

validation_rules.name: Voltage Drop Exceeds Limit

rule.description: Voltage drop must not exceed 10% of nominal voltage (2.4V for 24VDC system). Voltage at farthest device must be ≥ 16VDC (minimum operating voltage).

calculation.method: For each circuit: get total current (sum of device alarm currents), wire gauge resistance (Ω/kft), one-way wire length (ft). Calculate Vdrop = 2 × I × R × L / 1000. Check Vdrop ≤ 2.4V and V_panel - Vdrop ≥ 16V.

validation_rules.error_message: Circuit {circuit_label}: voltage drop {actual_drop}V exceeds {max_drop}V — voltage at farthest device is {device_voltage}V (minimum required: 16V)
validation_rules.resolution: Increase wire gauge, shorten wire run, or add another circuit

validation_rules.name: SLC Device Count Exceeds Loop Maximum

rule.description: Total number of devices on an SLC loop must not exceed the panel manufacturer's maximum device count per loop.

calculation.method: Count all devices assigned to this SLC loop. Compare against panel max_devices_per_loop. If count > max → FAIL.

validation_rules.error_message: SLC {circuit_label}: {count} devices exceeds maximum {max} devices per loop for panel {panel_model}
validation_rules.resolution: Add another SLC loop or redistribute devices

validation_rules.name: Conventional Zone Device Count Exceeds 20

rule.description: Conventional zones should have maximum 20 devices per zone for reliable supervision and fault identification.

calculation.method: Count devices per zone/circuit. If count > 20 → WARNING.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Zone {zone_label} has {count} devices — recommended maximum is 20 for conventional zones

validation_rules.name: Zone Area Exceeds 2000m² (BS 5839)

rule.description: No fire alarm zone should exceed 2000m² area (BS 5839-1 Section 23.2).

calculation.method: Sum areas of all rooms assigned to this zone. If total > 2000 → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Zone {zone_label} area ({actual} m²) exceeds maximum 2000 m² (BS 5839-1 Section 23.2)
validation_rules.resolution: Split into multiple zones

validation_rules.name: Zone Covers Multiple Floors (BS 5839)

rule.description: One zone should ideally not cover more than one floor (unless floor area < 300m²).

calculation.method: Count distinct floors assigned to this zone. If > 1 AND zone area > 300m² → WARNING.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Zone {zone_label} covers {count} floors — BS 5839 recommends one zone per floor (BS 5839-1 Section 23.2)

validation_rules.name: Stairwell Not in Separate Zone (BS 5839)

rule.description: Enclosed stairwells should be in a separate zone from floor areas.
rule.requirement: Stairwell devices should be in their own dedicated zone

calculation.method: Check if any stairwell has detectors assigned to a zone that also contains non-stairwell areas. If yes → WARNING.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Stairwell detectors share zone with floor area — should be in dedicated zone (BS 5839-1 Clause 23.2.5)

validation_rules.name: Battery Capacity Insufficient

rule.description: Battery must support 24-hour standby + 5-minute alarm (general) or 24-hour + 15-minute alarm (voice evacuation).

calculation.method: Sum standby_current_mA of ALL devices (24hr). Sum alarm_current_mA of ALL devices. Calculate required Ah. Compare against installed battery Ah. If installed < required → FAIL.

validation_rules.error_message: Battery capacity {installed} Ah is insufficient — required {required} Ah (NFPA 72 Section 10.6.7.2.1)
validation_rules.resolution: Increase battery size to {required_rounded} Ah or greater

validation_rules.name: Elevator Lobby Missing Smoke Detector

rule.description: Smoke detector required in each elevator lobby on every floor served by elevator for Phase 1 recall.
rule.requirement: One smoke detector in front of each elevator door on every floor

calculation.method: If building has_elevator=true, check each floor has at least one smoke detector near elevator lobby area. If any floor missing → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Floor {floor_name}: elevator lobby missing smoke detector — required for elevator Phase 1 recall (NFPA 72 Section 21.6)

validation_rules.name: Elevator Shaft Missing Smoke Detector

rule.description: Smoke detector should be installed at the top of each elevator hoistway (shaft). Required by ASME A17.1.

calculation.method: If building has elevators, check that hoistway smoke detectors are assigned. Flag if missing.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Elevator hoistway smoke detector not detected — verify installation at top of shaft (NFPA 72 Section 17.13.3)

validation_rules.name: Duct Detector Missing for Large HVAC

rule.description: Duct smoke detector required in supply and return air systems when HVAC system > 2000 CFM and serving more than one zone.

calculation.method: If building has HVAC with CFM > 2000, check that duct smoke detectors are specified for supply and return air systems.

validation_rules.error_message: HVAC system ({cfm} CFM > 2000 CFM) requires duct smoke detectors — not found (NFPA 72 Section 17.7.5.1)

validation_rules.name: Door Holders Not Connected

rule.description: Electromagnetic fire door holders must be connected to fire alarm system for release on alarm.
rule.requirement: All fire door holders must have interface module connection to fire alarm panel

calculation.method: If building has fire-rated doors with magnetic holders, check that control relay modules are specified for door release.

validation_rules.error_message: WARNING: Fire door holders detected but no release interface modules specified — doors will not release on alarm (NFPA 72 Section 21.9)

validation_rules.name: Sprinkler Monitoring Missing

rule.description: If building has sprinkler system, water flow switches and control valve tamper switches must be monitored by the fire alarm system.

calculation.method: If has_sprinkler=true, check that monitor modules are specified for water flow and valve tamper switches.

validation_rules.error_message: Sprinkler system detected but monitoring points missing — water flow and valve tamper must be monitored (NFPA 72 Section 21.5)

validation_rules.name: Floor Missing Detection

rule.description: Every floor of the building must have at least one detection device (or documented reason for no detection).
rule.minimum: At least one detector, pull station, or call point per floor

calculation.method: For each floor in the building, check that at least one device is assigned. If no devices on a floor → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Floor {floor_name} has NO fire alarm devices — every floor must have detection
validation_rules.resolution: Add at minimum one smoke detector and one pull station/call point

validation_rules.name: Panel Not Specified

rule.description: Every fire alarm system must have a control panel specified.
rule.requirement: At least one control panel must be assigned to the project

calculation.method: Check that project has at least one control panel device. If none → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: No fire alarm control panel (FACP) specified — required for every fire alarm system

validation_rules.name: Devices Without Zone Assignment

rule.description: Every device must be assigned to a zone.
rule.requirement: All detectors, pull stations, and modules must have a zone_id

calculation.method: Check all devices in the project. If any device has zone_id = null → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Device {device_tag} is not assigned to any zone — all devices must be zoned
validation_rules.resolution: Assign device to appropriate zone

validation_rules.name: Duplicate Device Address

rule.description: Each addressable device on the same SLC loop must have a unique address. Duplicate addresses cause communication failure.
rule.requirement: device_address must be unique within each slc_loop_id

calculation.method: For each SLC loop, check that no two devices share the same device_address. If duplicate found → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Duplicate address {address} on SLC loop {loop_id} — devices {device1} and {device2} have the same address
validation_rules.resolution: Change address of one device to resolve conflict

validation_rules.name: High-Rise Voice Evacuation Required

rule.description: Buildings with occupied floors > 75 ft (23m) above fire department access level MUST have emergency voice alarm communication system (EVACS).

calculation.method: If building.is_highrise=true, check that project has voice evacuation equipment specified (voice panel, speakers). If missing → ERROR.

validation_rules.error_message: Building is high-rise ({height}m > 23m) — emergency voice evacuation system is REQUIRED (NFPA 72 Chapter 24)
validation_rules.resolution: Specify voice evacuation panel and speakers on all floors

validation_checklist.description: Master checklist for design review — every item must pass before submission
